# ncurses C ‚Üí ncurses-native per-function parity (gap view)

C inventory provenance: Ubuntu clang version 18.1.3 (1ubuntu1) via clang -Xclang -ast-dump=json on installed ncursesw headers; headers: `curses.h` (456 fns, sha256 `bbdf40f38aef`), `term.h` (33 fns, sha256 `26e32c4d77dc`).

## How to read this (and what the percentage is NOT)

The percentage is a strict ratio: C functions that are *resolved* divided by all
C public API functions. Resolved means either a reconstructed byte producer
(full/partial/scaffold/divergent) or an evidence-backed non-output classification
(deferred = proven to emit no immediate bytes; n_a = a pure query with no
terminal-output contract). It is **not** a claim that a counterpart implements the
C function's signature, return values, or window semantics -- ncurses-native is a
byte-output reconstruction, not a C API. Credit is withheld from every function
that is neither reconstructed nor classified with evidence.

**Overall: 481 / 481 C functions are resolved (100.0%). The other 0 are gaps.**

Legend: `*` full = every function in the group reconstructed and admitted by a court ¬∑ `~` partial = some behavior reconstructed with an executable court (byte/terminfo oracle) ¬∑ `o` scaffold = a byte-producing stand-in exists, not yet pinned by its own court ¬∑ `!` divergent = a counterpart exists but a court records a measured divergence from the in-repo oracle ¬∑ `>` deferred = no immediate terminal output; the function's effect is realized later by refresh/doupdate, proven by an empty-output court ¬∑ `n` n_a = no terminal-output contract (pure query or state); nothing for a byte-output reconstruction to emit ¬∑ `.` none = no counterpart.

### Bell (curs_beep) ‚Äî 4/4 (100.0%)

- `*` `beep` (full) ‚Üí `bell::beep` [NCURSES.CAP.BEL] ‚Äî Emits the bel capability (xterm: 0x07); a pure output cap with no window state.
- `*` `beep_sp` (full) ‚Üí `bell::beep` [NCURSES.CAP.BEL] ‚Äî Byte-identical to beep; the _sp screen selection is not modeled but emits the same bel byte.
- `*` `flash` (full) ‚Üí `bell::flash` [NCURSES.CAP.FLASH] ‚Äî Emits the flash capability; the $<100/> delay is a real-time pause, not bytes, so the stream is the DECSCNM on/off pair.
- `*` `flash_sp` (full) ‚Üí `bell::flash` [NCURSES.CAP.FLASH] ‚Äî Byte-identical to flash; the _sp screen selection is not modeled.

- `*` `mvcur` (full) ‚Üí `cursor::mvcur` [NCURSES.MVCUR, NCURSES.CAP.CUP, NCURSES.CAP.CUU1, NCURSES.CAP.HOME] ‚Äî Clean-room reproduction of lib_mvcur.c's onscreen_mvcur tactic enumeration scored by relative_move with the static sample-parameter cost model from _nc_mvcur_init (strict-< tie-break, ovw=FALSE per public mvcur(3)). Verified byte-exact against real libncurses for ALL 23409 pairs of a 153x153 sampled (from -> to) grid on an 80x24 xterm pty (court NCURSES.MVCUR); the offline replay is tests/mvcur_matrix.rs.

### Refresh & doupdate (curs_refresh) ‚Äî 7/7 (100.0%)

- `~` `doupdate` (partial) ‚Üí `update::Screen::doupdate` [NCURSES.DOUPDATE] ‚Äî Clean-room reproduction of tty_update.c (doupdate/ClrUpdate/TransformLine/PutRange/EmitRange/ClrToEOL/ClrBottom) for the plain-text (A_NORMAL, no color) path, reusing the byte-exact mvcur/mvcur_ovw for cursor motion. Byte-exact vs real ncurses on ALL 19 court scenarios (NCURSES.DOUPDATE): clear+paint, overwrite, multi-line, scattered runs, leading-blank reposition, clr_eol/clr_bol/clr_eos, ich/dch insert/delete shifting, ech and rep run-coalescing, and the mvcur ovw=TRUE GoTo overwrite (OPT-01a closed). Attribute/color SGR (OPT-03), xmc, bce, hardware scroll, and the right-margin wrap corner are out of this increment.
- `o` `doupdate_sp` (scaffold) ‚Üí `screen::single_field_repaint` ‚Äî Same single-shape repaint seed as doupdate; the _sp screen-pointer variant is not modelled.
- `>` `redrawwin` (deferred) [NCURSES.REFRESH.NOOUTPUT] ‚Äî Marks a window/line range for full repaint on the next refresh; emits no immediate terminal bytes (proven empty-output).
- `~` `refresh` (partial) ‚Üí `update::Screen::doupdate` [NCURSES.DOUPDATE] ‚Äî wrefresh(stdscr) = wnoutrefresh + doupdate; the doupdate plain-text screen-diff is reproduced byte-exact for the courted scenarios (NCURSES.DOUPDATE). The wnoutrefresh window-to-newscr copy and pad/sub-window clipping are modeled by the Window cell grid, not yet courted end-to-end.
- `~` `wnoutrefresh` (partial) ‚Üí `update::Screen::doupdate` [NCURSES.DOUPDATE] ‚Äî The newscr assembly feeds the same byte-exact doupdate diff (NCURSES.DOUPDATE). Full WINDOW-to-newscr copy semantics (clipping, leaveok, immedok) are partial.
- `>` `wredrawln` (deferred) [NCURSES.REFRESH.NOOUTPUT] ‚Äî Marks a window/line range for full repaint on the next refresh; emits no immediate terminal bytes (proven empty-output).
- `~` `wrefresh` (partial) ‚Üí `update::Screen::doupdate` [NCURSES.DOUPDATE] ‚Äî wnoutrefresh + doupdate; the doupdate plain-text screen-diff is byte-exact for the courted scenarios (NCURSES.DOUPDATE), reusing the byte-exact mvcur.
